content.problemStatement:
Given an array  A1,A2,..,ANA_1, A_2, .., A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​  of size NNN and an integer KKK.

You want to choose KKK elements from array AAA such that difference between maximum and minimum element ( from chosen elements) is minimum.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2*10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the minimum possible difference between the maximum and minimum among any chosen K elements.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we sort the array and examine all windows of size K. The minimal differences are 3, 1 and 39 respectively.
All elements are equal, any two chosen have max‑min = 0.
Sorted array: 1 2 3 4 5 6 8. Windows of size 5 give differences 4, 4 and 5; the minimum is 4.

solution.approach: Sort the array. After sorting, any K chosen elements that give the minimal range must be a contiguous segment of length K. Scan all windows of size K and keep the smallest difference A[i+K-1] - A[i].
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case for sorting, O(N) for the sliding window. Overall O(total N log N).
